title: Titan Submersible — Marine Board of Investigation, Report of Investigation (U.S. Coast Guard)
organization: U.S. Coast Guard — Marine Board of Investigation
bias_or_interest: Government casualty investigation. Authoritative for cause, contributing factors, and the 'preventable' finding.
relevant_locations: Primary contributing factors: inadequate design/certification/maintenance/inspection; toxic workplace culture
Experimental carbon-fiber/titanium hull; bonding voids/defects
Ignored hull delamination after Dive 80 (July 2022); acoustic anomalies
Casualty was 'preventable'; 17 safety recommendations; 300+ pages
quotation_note: Government work; factual. Attribute cause findings to the Coast Guard Board.
notes: MBI Report of Investigation released 2025-08-05. The spine of Movement 5; makes cause record-based.

title: Titan submersible implosion — Date, Cause, OceanGate, Location, Timeline, Deaths, & Map (Britannica)
bias_or_interest: Authoritative encyclopaedia aggregator. Used for the who/when/where and overall framing.
relevant_locations: Five aboard; June 18, 2023; North Atlantic off Newfoundland
Dive to the RMS Titanic wreck
quotation_note: Summarize; cross-checked against USCG and primary reporting.

title: Titan submersible implosion (Wikipedia)
bias_or_interest: Neutral aggregator. Used for dive timeline detail (loss of contact 1h33m), debris location (~500m from Titanic bow), 4-day search.
relevant_locations: Loss of contact 1 hour 33 minutes into the dive
Debris field found June 22, 2023, ~500 m from the Titanic's bow
Search June 18-22, 2023

title: 'All good here': Last messages revealed from Titan submersible before implosion (ABC News, Coast Guard hearing)
bias_or_interest: Major outlet reporting USCG hearing exhibits. Source for the last text messages.
relevant_locations: 'All good here' (~2,274 m)
Last message 'dropped two wts' (drop weights, ~3,341 m)
quotation_note: Quote the two short messages exactly; attribute to the Coast Guard hearing record.
rights_note: News report of public hearing exhibits.
notes: The last words; cross-check with SRC-0001.

title: Titan submersible maker OceanGate faced safety lawsuit in 2018: 'Potential danger to passengers' (CBS News)
bias_or_interest: Major outlet quoting 2018 court filings. Source for David Lochridge's warnings, the QC report, and the lawsuit.
relevant_locations: Lochridge fired Jan 2018 after QC report
Carbon-fiber hull concerns; certified ~1,300 m vs intended ~4,000 m
OSHA whistleblower complaint; OceanGate sued him
notes: Whistleblower spine of Movement 2; cross-check with SRC-0006.

title: A whistleblower raised safety concerns about OceanGate's submersible in 2018. Then he was fired. (TechCrunch)
bias_or_interest: Major tech outlet on the Lochridge matter; corroborates SRC-0005.
relevant_locations: Lochridge raised safety concerns 2018, then fired
Disagreement over non-destructive testing

title: Marine Technology Society letter to OceanGate Inc., March 27, 2018 (USCG exhibit CG-068, redacted)
organization: Marine Technology Society (via U.S. Coast Guard exhibit)
bias_or_interest: Primary document: industry-body warning letter, entered as a Coast Guard hearing exhibit. Authoritative for the 2018 expert warning.
relevant_locations: 'current experimental approach ... could result in negative outcomes (from minor to catastrophic)'
Concern: not pursuing recognized certification (DNV-GL); representation 'misleading'
Reflected concerns of three dozen-plus experts
quotation_note: Quote 'minor to catastrophic' phrase exactly; attribute to the March 27, 2018 MTS letter.
notes: The 'they were warned' chorus; primary document. Re-verify exact exhibit URL at publish time.

title: Teen on doomed Titanic sub couldn't wait to set Rubik's Cube record during trip, his mother says (CBS News)
bias_or_interest: Major outlet reporting Christine Dawood's BBC interview. Source for Suleman, the Rubik's Cube, and that she gave up her place.
relevant_locations: Suleman Dawood, 19; brought a Rubik's Cube to set a depth record
Christine Dawood gave up her own place to her son
quotation_note: Attribute to the mother's interview; handle with restraint and dignity.
rights_note: News report of a family interview; living relative — documented statements only.
notes: Emotional spine. DIGNITY: documented only; no invented dialogue.

title: Teen who died on Titanic sub brought Rubik's Cube in bid to break world record (NBC News)
bias_or_interest: Major outlet; reports an aunt's account that Suleman was 'terrified' but went over Father's Day to please his father.
relevant_locations: Suleman reportedly 'terrified' to board
Went over Father's Day weekend to please his father
quotation_note: Attribute 'terrified' to the relative's account ('reportedly'); do not state as omniscient fact.
rights_note: News report; living relatives — documented statements only.
notes: Corroborates SRC-0008 on the boy; attribute carefully.

title: Stockton Rush — quotations (Wikiquote), incl. 2022 CBS Sunday Morning interview with David Pogue
bias_or_interest: Quotation aggregator; used to locate the verifiable 'At some point, safety just is pure waste' (2022, David Pogue) and 'rules you break' statements.
relevant_locations: 'At some point, safety just is pure waste.' (2022 interview, David Pogue)
'remembered for the rules you break' framing
quotation_note: Use only quotes traceable to the named on-record interview; verify wording against the CBS Sunday Morning source at publish time.
rights_note: Quotation of a public figure's recorded on-record statements (deceased).
notes: Thesis-stating quote (the villain names the theme). Re-verify exact wording/source before publish.
